title: compiler-api: extract shared CompilerComponent surface
description:
Context:
- No cross-platform compiler API module exists; each platform (JVM, JS, Native, WASM) has no shared contract surface to depend on.
- Both playground UIs (Laminar and SvelteKit/WASM) need a stable, platform-agnostic CompilerComponent trait to depend on, backed by PureLogic effects.
- Without this module, webapp, webapp-wasm, and the cross-platform equivalence test cannot be built.

EARS requirements:
- REQ-compilerapi-001 (When): When a consumer calls CompilerComponent.parseCst/parseAst/parseQuery/runQuery, the system shall return a CompileEff[A] value backed by PureLogic QueryEffect, wrapping existing Krueger.parseCst, QueryParser.parse, Matcher.matches, and QueryPretty.render calls.
- REQ-compilerapi-002 (If): If the underlying parse or query operation fails, then the system shall accumulate a structured CompileError (sealed ADT: ParseError, QueryError, InternalError) in the PureLogic Result envelope rather than throwing.
- REQ-compilerapi-003 (Where): Where a caller runs the effect via CompilerComponent.run, the system shall return a CompileResult[A] containing context, logs, errors, and value — the Logic type itself never crossing the JS/WASM FFI boundary.

Acceptance:
1) Implementation expectations:
   - New krueger/compiler-api/ module cross-compiled to JVM, JS, Native, and WASM.
   - moduleDeps: krueger.core + krueger.trees (PureLogic arrives transitively via trees).
   - Public surface: CompilerComponent trait, CompileError sealed ADT, MatchView case class, CompilerComponent companion with type aliases (CompileCtx, CompileLog, CompileErr, CompileEff[A], CompileResult[A]) and inline run helper.
   - Default implementation Krueger.component wiring existing pure APIs inside PureLogic effects.
   - Mill module registration in mill-build/src/build/Modules.scala and build.mill.yaml.
2) Test matrix (mandatory):
   - happy-path: valid Elm source produces non-empty CstModule; valid query on that CST produces MatchView list.
   - negative/failure: malformed source produces ParseError in errors; malformed query produces QueryError in errors; value is None/empty and no exception thrown.
   - edge/boundary: empty source, empty query, query matching zero nodes each return well-formed Result with empty value/matches.
   - regression: none yet — this is new.
3) User-visible behavior:
   - Not directly visible; unblocks webapp and webapp-wasm.
   - Unit tests on JVM confirm the surface before JS/WASM builds.
4) Determinism and diagnostics:
   - CompileResult logs and errors arrays are ordered deterministically (append order from PureLogic).
   - CompileError cases carry phase, message, and optional span for actionable diagnostics.
close_reason: Extracted krueger.compiler-api cross-compiled module (JVM+JS+Native). CompilerComponent trait + CompileError sealed ADT + MatchView (moved from itest) + Krueger.component default impl wrapping parseCst/parseAst/QueryParser/Matcher/QueryPretty inside PureLogic effects. 12 tests pass on JVM and JS. WASM variant deferred to -3hy.

title: ci: build both webapps + SvelteKit static output; stitch into Pages artifact
description:
Context:
- The GitHub Pages deploy workflow currently only builds the Astro/Starlight docs site; it needs to also run the Mill webapp tasks and SvelteKit static build, then stitch the SvelteKit output into docs/dist/try-wasm/ before uploading the Pages artifact.
- Without this, the /try-wasm/ playground is never deployed even if all code is merged.

EARS requirements:
- REQ-ci-001 (When): When the deploy workflow runs, the system shall invoke ./mill webapp.writeToDocsPublic webapp-wasm.writeToWasmSite before the Astro build so both JS and WASM artifacts are present in docs/public/ and sites/try-wasm/static/wasm/ respectively.
- REQ-ci-002 (When): When the SvelteKit build completes, the system shall copy sites/try-wasm/build/ into docs/dist/try-wasm/ so the single Pages artifact upload at docs/dist covers both sites.
- REQ-ci-003 (If): If any Mill task or SvelteKit build step fails, then the workflow shall fail fast and not upload a partial Pages artifact.

Acceptance:
1) Implementation expectations:
   - Update .github/workflows/deploy.yml: add step running ./mill webapp.writeToDocsPublic webapp-wasm.writeToWasmSite (or aggregate alias).
   - Add step: cd sites/try-wasm && npm ci && npm run build.
   - Add step: copy sites/try-wasm/build/** to docs/dist/try-wasm/.
   - Add Node cache entry for sites/try-wasm/package-lock.json.
   - No new jobs; reuse existing Mill + Coursier + Node caches.
   - Single Pages artifact upload at docs/dist unchanged.
2) Test matrix (mandatory):
   - happy-path: full deploy workflow completes; Pages artifact contains /try/ and /try-wasm/ directories.
   - negative/failure: Mill task failure causes workflow to fail at that step (not silently continue).
   - negative/failure: SvelteKit build failure fails the workflow before artifact upload.
   - edge/boundary: running Mill tasks and SvelteKit build in sequence (not parallel) avoids file-write races on sites/try-wasm/static/wasm/.
   - regression: existing Scaladoc build and Astro build steps unaffected.
3) User-visible behavior:
   - CI gate and deploy mechanism; verify by downloading Pages artifact and checking /try-wasm/ path exists.
4) Determinism and diagnostics:
   - Workflow step ordering is deterministic: Mill tasks → SvelteKit build → Astro build → stitch → upload.
   - Workflow fails with a clear step name if any stage errors.

title: docs: full-bleed /try/ page + bootstrap
description:
Context:
- The Astro docs site needs a full-bleed /try/ route (bypassing Starlight's sidebar/TOC chrome) that mounts the Laminar webapp bundle, configures Monaco workers via Vite, and sizes the playground to fill the viewport below the header.

EARS requirements:
- REQ-docs-trypage-001 (When): When a user navigates to /try/, the system shall render a full-bleed page with the Laminar playground mounted in a div sized to calc(100vh - var(--header-h)), with Monaco editors active for source and query inputs.
- REQ-docs-trypage-002 (If): If the Scala.js webapp bundle (docs/public/try/app/main.js) is absent, then the page shall show a loading placeholder or error message rather than a blank/broken layout.
- REQ-docs-trypage-003 (Where): Where the /try/ page uses Astro pages/ (not content/docs/), the system shall bypass Starlight's sidebar and TOC chrome while still rendering the shared Header.astro component.

Acceptance:
1) Implementation expectations:
   - New docs/src/pages/try.astro: imports Header.astro, renders <div id="try-krueger-root">, includes client:only script importing bootstrap.ts.
   - New docs/src/playground/bootstrap.ts: loads Monaco workers (ESM ?worker imports), imports webapp ESModule, calls Main.mount('#try-krueger-root').
   - docs/astro.config.mjs: Vite config for Monaco workers (optimizeDeps, worker.format: 'es').
   - docs/package.json: monaco-editor added to dependencies.
   - Page-scoped CSS: CSS Grid for activity bar / editor group / panel, using --sl-color-* tokens.
2) Test matrix (mandatory):
   - happy-path: npm run build:full produces docs/dist/try/index.html with correct structure and no 404 asset refs.
   - negative/failure: missing webapp bundle causes bootstrap.ts import to fail with a clear console error, not a silent blank page.
   - edge/boundary: page loads correctly with JavaScript disabled (shows a noscript fallback message).
   - regression: adding /try/ page does not break existing docs pages or Starlight routing.
3) User-visible behavior:
   - Core playground entry point; visual verification on local dev and production build required.
4) Determinism and diagnostics:
   - Bootstrap script load order is deterministic (Monaco workers before app mount).
   - Page height calculation via --header-h CSS var is stable across Starlight version updates.

title: docs: custom Starlight Header override with Try dropdown
description:
Context:
- The Starlight docs site needs a custom Header component that adds a 'Try' dropdown (or two nav siblings) linking to both playground flavors (/try/ and /try-wasm/) while preserving existing Starlight nav items (Docs, API, GitHub, theme toggle, search).
- Without this, users cannot discover the playgrounds from the docs nav.

EARS requirements:
- REQ-docs-header-001 (When): When a user visits any docs page, the system shall display a top nav containing: Docs link, Try dropdown (Laminar → /try/, WASM → /try-wasm/), API link, GitHub icon link, theme toggle, and search.
- REQ-docs-header-002 (If): If the current page path starts with /try, then the Try nav item shall have an active-route highlight applied; if specifically /try-wasm/, the WASM sub-item shall also be highlighted.
- REQ-docs-header-003 (Where): Where the Header.astro component is registered via components.Header in starlight.config.ts, Starlight's SiteTitle, Search, SocialIcons, and ThemeSelect subcomponents shall be reused rather than reimplemented.

Acceptance:
1) Implementation expectations:
   - New docs/src/components/Header.astro with logo, nav links, dropdown, theme toggle, GitHub icon.
   - Registered in docs/starlight.config.ts via components: { Header: './src/components/Header.astro' }.
   - Active-route logic: Astro.url.pathname.startsWith('/try') for the Try item; exact /try-wasm/ check for the WASM sub-item.
   - Reuse Starlight's built-in subcomponents (SiteTitle, Search, SocialIcons, ThemeSelect) where possible.
2) Test matrix (mandatory):
   - happy-path: on a docs page, Try dropdown renders with both Laminar and WASM entries.
   - negative/failure: if starlight.config.ts components.Header is missing or misconfigured, build fails with a clear Astro error, not a silent blank nav.
   - edge/boundary: very long page title does not cause header layout overflow.
   - regression: existing Docs/API/GitHub links remain functional after Header override.
3) User-visible behavior:
   - Visible on every docs page; unit test via Astro component testing or visual snapshot.
4) Determinism and diagnostics:
   - Active-route highlight is deterministic based on Astro.url.pathname at build time for static pages.

title: mill: webapp.writeToDocsPublic + webapp-wasm.writeToWasmSite tasks
description:
Context:
- Mill needs output-copy tasks analogous to the existing docs.writeToDocsPublic (Scaladoc copy) pattern so that fullLinkJS artifacts from webapp and webapp-wasm land in the correct locations for the Astro and SvelteKit builds respectively.
- Without these tasks, CI cannot orchestrate a single build command that produces all artifacts.

EARS requirements:
- REQ-mill-tasks-001 (When): When webapp.writeToDocsPublic is invoked, the system shall run fullLinkJS and copy main.js and source map to docs/public/try/app/, mirroring the existing docs.writeToDocsPublic pattern.
- REQ-mill-tasks-002 (When): When webapp-wasm.writeToWasmSite is invoked, the system shall run the WASM linker and copy main.wasm plus the ES loader to sites/try-wasm/static/wasm/.
- REQ-mill-tasks-003 (If): If either copy task is included in an aggregate alias (e.g. site.buildAll), then invoking the alias shall run both tasks and all their transitive deps without requiring manual task ordering.

Acceptance:
1) Implementation expectations:
   - webapp.writeToDocsPublic task in build.mill.yaml (or Modules.scala): runs fullLinkJS, copies output to docs/public/try/app/.
   - webapp-wasm.writeToWasmSite task: runs WASM fullLinkJS, copies main.wasm + loader to sites/try-wasm/static/wasm/.
   - Aggregate alias (site.buildAll or extension of existing docs.site) covers both tasks plus Scaladoc.
   - docs/package.json scripts: build:webapp and build:full added.
   - sites/try-wasm/package.json scripts: build:wasm and build:full added.
2) Test matrix (mandatory):
   - happy-path: ./mill webapp.writeToDocsPublic produces docs/public/try/app/main.js (non-zero size).
   - happy-path: ./mill webapp-wasm.writeToWasmSite produces sites/try-wasm/static/wasm/main.wasm (non-zero size).
   - negative/failure: invoking writeToDocsPublic when compiler-api.js fails propagates the error clearly.
   - edge/boundary: running both tasks in parallel (./mill webapp.writeToDocsPublic webapp-wasm.writeToWasmSite) completes without conflict.
   - regression: existing docs.writeToDocsPublic (Scaladoc) task unaffected.
3) User-visible behavior:
   - CI and local dev workflow; not directly user-visible.
4) Determinism and diagnostics:
   - Output files are deterministically overwritten on each run (no stale artifacts from previous builds).

title: sites/try-wasm: Vitest suite for krueger.ts wrapper
description:
Context:
- The TypeScript wrapper (src/lib/krueger.ts) over the WASM exports needs a Vitest suite to verify happy/negative/edge/determinism behavior against the real WASM bundle — no mocks of Krueger itself.
- Without this suite, regressions in the FFI envelope shape or serialization logic go undetected until manual testing.

EARS requirements:
- REQ-trywasm-vitest-001 (When): When krueger.ts wrapper functions are called with valid inputs in Vitest, the system shall return envelopes with ok=true and non-empty value fields matching expected structure.
- REQ-trywasm-vitest-002 (If): If the WASM Krueger.parseCst is called with malformed Elm, then the krueger.ts wrapper shall return an envelope with ok=false and a non-empty errors array without throwing.
- REQ-trywasm-vitest-003 (Where): Where the real WASM bundle is loaded via jsdom-compatible glue, the tests shall exercise the actual compiled Scala logic — no mock substitutions for Krueger exports.

Acceptance:
1) Implementation expectations:
   - Vitest suite at sites/try-wasm/src/lib/krueger.test.ts (or similar).
   - Tests load the real WASM bundle from static/wasm/ (populated by Mill before test run).
   - Covers: parseCst, parseAst, parseQuery, runQuery, prettyQuery.
   - npm test script in package.json runs vitest.
2) Test matrix (mandatory):
   - happy-path: valid Elm + valid query → ok=true, non-empty value, empty errors/logs.
   - negative/failure: malformed source → ok=false, errors non-empty, no thrown exception.
   - negative/failure: valid source + malformed query → ok=false on runQuery, errors describe query issue.
   - edge/boundary: empty string inputs → well-formed envelope (never throws).
   - regression: envelope shape { ok, value?, logs, errors } is stable across WASM rebuilds.
3) User-visible behavior:
   - CI gate; not directly user-visible.
4) Determinism and diagnostics:
   - Match order in runQuery results is asserted explicitly (same order as JVM/Laminar for same input).
   - Test output identifies which wrapper function and which envelope field failed.

title: sites/try-wasm: Svelte components mirroring Laminar UX
description:
Context:
- The SvelteKit playground needs the same VS Code-inspired UI as the Laminar version: ActivityBar, EditorGroup (dual Monaco), ResultsPanel with Matches/CST/AST/PrettyQuery tabs, and a collapsible TreeView — all wired to Svelte 5 runes state.
- The UX and CSS tokens must mirror the Laminar version so both playgrounds feel like one product.

EARS requirements:
- REQ-trywasm-components-001 (When): When the user interacts with the ActivityBar in the SvelteKit playground, the system shall switch the ResultsPanel to the selected output tab (Matches, CST, AST, or Canonical query echo) using Svelte 5 $state/$derived reactivity.
- REQ-trywasm-components-002 (If): If the WASM Krueger.runQuery call returns errors, then ResultsPanel shall display a human-readable error message instead of an empty tree or blank panel.
- REQ-trywasm-components-003 (Where): Where CSS custom properties use Starlight tokens (--sl-color-*), the SvelteKit components shall import the same token file as the Astro components so light/dark theming is visually identical.

Acceptance:
1) Implementation expectations:
   - ActivityBar.svelte, EditorGroup.svelte, ResultsPanel.svelte, MatchesView.svelte, TreeView.svelte, PrettyQueryView.svelte in src/lib/components/.
   - State: $state for source, query, selectedPanel; $derived for cstResult, astResult, matchResult via krueger.ts wrapper.
   - Monaco initialized in onMount inside EditorGroup.svelte; same Elm Monarch tokenizer as Laminar version.
   - CSS Grid layout matching Laminar version; Starlight --sl-color-* tokens for theming.
2) Test matrix (mandatory):
   - happy-path: ActivityBar click updates selectedPanel; ResultsPanel renders correct component.
   - negative/failure: error in cstResult renders error message in TreeView slot.
   - edge/boundary: empty match list renders 'no matches' placeholder; very large input does not hang.
   - regression: panel switch does not duplicate Monaco instances or cause memory leaks.
3) User-visible behavior:
   - Core /try-wasm/ playground UX; visual verification required alongside unit tests.
4) Determinism and diagnostics:
   - $derived computations are synchronous; no async races in Vitest tests.
   - TreeView expansion state is per-session (no persistence at v1).

title: sites/try-wasm: SvelteKit skeleton with adapter-static
description:
Context:
- A new SvelteKit 2 project is needed at sites/try-wasm/ to host the /try-wasm/ playground, consuming the WASM bundle from webapp-wasm and deploying as a static site alongside the Astro docs.
- The static adapter with the correct paths.base is required for GitHub Pages deployment under /krueger/try-wasm/.

EARS requirements:
- REQ-trywasm-skeleton-001 (When): When the SvelteKit project is built via vite build, the system shall emit a fully static site to sites/try-wasm/build/ with all assets relative to the /krueger/try-wasm base path.
- REQ-trywasm-skeleton-002 (If): If paths.base is set to /krueger/try-wasm in svelte.config.js, then all internal links and asset references shall be correctly prefixed for GitHub Pages deployment.
- REQ-trywasm-skeleton-003 (Where): Where the SvelteKit project depends on the WASM bundle, the static/wasm/ directory shall be populated by the Mill webapp-wasm.writeToWasmSite task before the SvelteKit build runs.

Acceptance:
1) Implementation expectations:
   - New sites/try-wasm/ directory: SvelteKit 2, Svelte 5 (runes), TypeScript, Vite.
   - @sveltejs/adapter-static configured with paths.base = '/krueger/try-wasm' and fallback: '404.html'.
   - package.json scripts: build:wasm (invokes Mill task), build:full (build:wasm && vite build).
   - tsconfig.json, svelte.config.js, vite.config.ts scaffolded correctly.
   - static/wasm/ directory present (populated by Mill; gitignored content, not the dir itself).
2) Test matrix (mandatory):
   - happy-path: npm run build:full completes without error; build/ contains index.html with correct base path.
   - negative/failure: missing static/wasm/main.wasm causes vite build to fail with a clear error, not a silent 404 at runtime.
   - edge/boundary: building with an empty static/wasm/ directory fails fast with an actionable message.
   - regression: adding new routes does not break the static adapter output.
3) User-visible behavior:
   - Not visible until components are added; this is the project scaffold.
4) Determinism and diagnostics:
   - Build output is reproducible across clean runs.
   - All asset paths deterministically prefixed with /krueger/try-wasm.

title: webapp-wasm: browser Wasm GC feature detection + fallback banner
description:
Context:
- Wasm GC is required for the Scala.js WASM backend (Chrome 119+, Firefox 120+, Safari 18.2+); older browsers will silently fail or show cryptic errors without a graceful fallback.
- A feature-detection script should detect Wasm GC availability at page load and show a banner directing users to /try/ (the Laminar playground) when unsupported.

EARS requirements:
- REQ-webappwasm-fallback-001 (When): When the browser does not support Wasm GC, the system shall render a visible banner on the /try-wasm/ page informing the user and providing a link to /try/ (the Laminar/Scala.js playground).
- REQ-webappwasm-fallback-002 (If): If the browser supports Wasm GC, then no banner shall be shown and the playground shall initialize normally.
- REQ-webappwasm-fallback-003 (Where): Where feature detection runs, the system shall use a synchronous or async check before Monaco and the WASM bundle initialize, so the banner appears before any failed WASM load.

Acceptance:
1) Implementation expectations:
   - Feature detection: check WebAssembly.validate with a minimal Wasm GC module bytes probe, or check navigator.userAgent version gates (probe preferred).
   - Banner: accessible, dismissable, links to /try/ with clear explanation.
   - Implemented in SvelteKit +page.svelte or a dedicated lib/wasm-detect.ts utility.
   - Documented in the /try-wasm/ page intro text.
2) Test matrix (mandatory):
   - happy-path: supported browser — no banner rendered; playground initializes.
   - negative/failure: unsupported browser (mocked via feature-detect stub) — banner renders with correct link to /try/.
   - edge/boundary: feature detection completes before WASM load attempt (order asserted in tests).
   - regression: banner does not appear in supported browsers after a WASM load failure unrelated to Wasm GC.
3) User-visible behavior:
   - Banner visible in unsupported browsers; tested via Vitest + jsdom feature-detect mock.
4) Determinism and diagnostics:
   - Detection result is stable per page load (no race between detection and WASM init).
   - Banner includes browser requirement text (Chrome 119+, Firefox 120+, Safari 18.2+).

title: webapp-wasm: Scala.js WASM linker config + JS exports
description:
Context:
- A dedicated module is needed to emit the WebAssembly artifact consumed by SvelteKit/try-wasm; this module has no Laminar dependency — it is purely a compiler-api wrapper with @JSExportTopLevel bindings.
- The @JSExportTopLevel wrappers must run PureLogic effects at the FFI boundary and serialize Result into plain-JS POJOs so SvelteKit can consume them without a Scala facade.

EARS requirements:
- REQ-webappwasm-001 (When): When KruegerJs.parseCst/parseAst/parseQuery/runQuery/prettyQuery is called from JavaScript, the system shall run the PureLogic CompileEff at the boundary and return a plain-JS envelope { ok: boolean, value?: any, logs: string[], errors: CompileError[] }.
- REQ-webappwasm-002 (If): If the CompileEff produces errors, then the envelope shall have ok=false and errors shall be a non-empty array of serialized CompileError objects; value shall be absent or null.
- REQ-webappwasm-003 (Where): Where the WASM linker config is active (withExperimentalUseWebAssembly(true)), the system shall emit both main.wasm and an ES-module JS loader without altering the standard compiler-api.js linker.

Acceptance:
1) Implementation expectations:
   - New krueger/webapp-wasm/ module: CommonScalaJSModule with scalaJSLinkerConfig overriding withExperimentalUseWebAssembly(true).
   - moduleDeps: krueger.compiler-api.js.
   - @JSExportTopLevel("Krueger") object KruegerJs with methods: parseCst, parseAst, parseQuery, runQuery, prettyQuery.
   - All methods run the effect via CompilerComponent.run and call toPojo helpers; the Logic type never crosses the JS boundary.
   - Envelope always has logs and errors arrays (possibly empty) for uniform SvelteKit consumption.
   - Mill task webapp-wasm.writeToWasmSite copies main.wasm + loader to sites/try-wasm/static/wasm/.
2) Test matrix (mandatory):
   - happy-path: calling KruegerJs.parseCst with valid Elm returns { ok: true, value: {...}, logs: [], errors: [] }.
   - negative/failure: malformed source returns { ok: false, errors: [{phase, message}], logs: [], value: null }.
   - edge/boundary: empty string input returns well-formed envelope (ok may be false, but shape is always { ok, value?, logs, errors }).
   - regression: envelope shape is stable — no extra fields, no missing fields across refactors.
3) User-visible behavior:
   - Not directly visible; consumed by SvelteKit site and Vitest suite.
4) Determinism and diagnostics:
   - logs and errors arrays are ordered by append order (deterministic).
   - toPojo helpers tested independently for serialization correctness.

title: webapp: ActivityBar + ResultsPanel + TreeView components
description:
Context:
- The /try/ playground needs VS Code-style UI components: an icon rail (ActivityBar), a tab panel switching between Matches/CST/AST/PrettyQuery (ResultsPanel), and a reusable collapsible tree view (TreeView) powered by the QueryableTree typeclass.
- These are the primary user-facing output components.

EARS requirements:
- REQ-webapp-components-001 (When): When the user clicks an ActivityBar icon, the system shall update the selectedPanel Var and ResultsPanel shall render the corresponding output (Matches, CST tree, AST tree, or Canonical query echo).
- REQ-webapp-components-002 (If): If the CompileResult for the selected panel contains errors, then ResultsPanel shall render a human-readable error message instead of an empty tree or blank view.
- REQ-webapp-components-003 (Where): Where TreeView renders a CST or AST node, the system shall use the existing QueryableTree typeclass instances (same ones used in tests) rather than custom traversal logic.

Acceptance:
1) Implementation expectations:
   - components/ActivityBar.scala: icon rail with four icons; emits panel-selection events to AppState.selectedPanel Var.
   - components/ResultsPanel.scala: switches on selectedPanel Signal to render MatchesView, TreeView (CST), TreeView (AST), or PrettyQueryView.
   - components/TreeView.scala: generic collapsible tree using QueryableTree; reuses same typeclass instances as itest/TestDriver.
   - components/MatchesView.scala: renders List[MatchView] with capture tag annotations.
   - components/PrettyQueryView.scala: renders QueryPretty output in a <pre>.
   - CSS via Starlight CSS custom properties (--sl-color-*) for light/dark theming.
2) Test matrix (mandatory):
   - happy-path: ActivityBar click updates selectedPanel; ResultsPanel renders correct child component.
   - negative/failure: CompileError in cstResult renders error message in TreeView slot, not blank.
   - edge/boundary: empty match list renders a 'no matches' placeholder, not a blank panel.
   - regression: switching panels does not cause signal glitches or duplicate subscriptions.
3) User-visible behavior:
   - Core playground UX; both unit tests (signal wiring) and visual verification on /try/ required.
4) Determinism and diagnostics:
   - Panel switch is synchronous; no async races in tests.
   - TreeView node expansion state is per-session (no persistence required at v1).

title: webapp: Monaco facade + Elm Monarch tokenizer
description:
Context:
- The /try/ playground uses Monaco Editor for source and query editing; we need a minimal Scala.js @JSImport facade and an Elm Monarch tokenizer registered on load.
- The tokenizer keyword list must stay in sync with Krueger's own lexer to avoid highlighting divergence.

EARS requirements:
- REQ-webapp-monaco-001 (When): When MonacoFacade initializes, the system shall register the Elm Monarch tokenizer with Monaco so Elm source syntax highlighting is active in the source editor.
- REQ-webapp-monaco-002 (If): If the Monaco worker scripts fail to load, then the system shall degrade gracefully (editor still functional, syntax highlighting absent) without throwing an unhandled exception.
- REQ-webapp-monaco-003 (Where): Where the Elm keyword list in ElmLanguage.scala diverges from krueger/core lexer keywords, a compile-time or test-time assertion shall surface the mismatch.

Acceptance:
1) Implementation expectations:
   - monaco/MonacoFacade.scala: minimal @JSImport facade covering editor.create, setValue, getValue, onDidChangeModelContent, and language registration APIs.
   - monaco/ElmLanguage.scala: Monarch tokenizer definition with keyword/operator list cribbed from krueger/core/src/io/eleven19/krueger/lexer/; registered on module load.
   - docs/package.json: add monaco-editor npm dep; docs/astro.config.mjs: Vite config for Monaco workers.
2) Test matrix (mandatory):
   - happy-path: ElmLanguage tokenizer compiles and registers without error; spot-check a few Elm keywords are in the tokenizer list.
   - negative/failure: facade @JSImport for a non-existent Monaco function fails at link time (not silently at runtime).
   - edge/boundary: registering the tokenizer twice does not throw (idempotent).
   - regression: tokenizer keyword list kept in sync via cross-reference test or shared source.
3) User-visible behavior:
   - Elm syntax highlighting in the source editor; visible in /try/ playground.
4) Determinism and diagnostics:
   - Tokenizer registration is idempotent.
   - Missing Monaco worker produces a console warning, not an unhandled rejection.
close_reason: Monaco facade (@JSImport) + ElmLanguage Monarch tokenizer landed. Keyword/operator sets sourced from ElmLexer — alignment pinned by 5 passing ZIO tests. register() is idempotent via getLanguages() check. docs/package.json monaco-editor npm dep deferred to docs bootstrap (-95s). Full suite green: 98 core + 12 compiler-api + 14 webapp tests.

title: webapp: AppState signals + tests
description:
Context:
- The webapp module needs a reactive state layer (AppState) that wires CompilerComponent outputs to Laminar Vars and Signals, so UI components can declaratively react to source/query changes.
- Without AppState, all UI components would need to manage parsing/matching state themselves, creating duplication and untestable logic.

EARS requirements:
- REQ-webapp-appstate-001 (When): When sourceVar or queryVar changes, the system shall derive new cstResult, astResult, and matchResult Signal values by calling CompilerComponent through the PureLogic effect layer.
- REQ-webapp-appstate-002 (If): If the source is malformed, then cstResult and astResult signals shall emit a CompileError value and matchResult shall emit an empty list, without crashing downstream signals.
- REQ-webapp-appstate-003 (Where): Where the query matches zero nodes, matchResult shall emit an empty List[MatchView] (not an error) so the UI can distinguish zero-matches from a query parse failure.

Acceptance:
1) Implementation expectations:
   - state/AppState.scala with Var[String] for source and query; derived Signal[CompileResult[CstModule]], Signal[CompileResult[AstModule]], Signal[List[MatchView]].
   - All derivation via CompilerComponent; no raw Krueger.parseCst calls in AppState.
   - RGR cycle: failing tests first, then implementation.
2) Test matrix (mandatory):
   - happy-path: valid source + valid query → non-empty matches, non-error CST/AST results.
   - negative/failure: malformed source → CST/AST signals carry CompileError; matchResult is empty list.
   - negative/failure: valid source + malformed query → CST/AST signals are success; matchResult carries query CompileError or empty list.
   - edge/boundary: empty source, empty query, very large source, query matching zero nodes — none crash signals.
   - regression: changing source then query in sequence produces expected final state (no stale signal).
3) User-visible behavior:
   - Visible via the /try/ playground; unit tests cover signal wiring directly.
4) Determinism and diagnostics:
   - Match order in matchResult is stable and asserted explicitly in tests.
   - Signal emissions are synchronous in tests (no async races).
close_reason: AppState implemented with 7 ZIO tests green: happy, 2 negative, 2 edge (empty + zero-match), regression (source→query sequence), determinism. All derivations flow through CompilerComponent (no raw Krueger.parseCst calls). Match list is empty when source or query is malformed, non-crash.

title: compiler-api: cross-platform equivalence test (JVM/JS/Native/WASM)
description:
Context:
- The promise of compiler-api is that all four platforms (JVM, JS, Native, WASM) return structurally identical Result values for the same input.
- Without a cross-platform test suite, this contract is untested and regressions go undetected.
- This suite is the authoritative proof that the shared-component architecture works end-to-end.

EARS requirements:
- REQ-compilerapi-xtest-001 (When): When the cross-platform compiler-api.test suite runs on JVM, JS, Native, and WASM, the system shall produce structurally equal CompileResult values (context, logs, errors, value) for each fixed fixture input across all four platforms.
- REQ-compilerapi-xtest-002 (If): If one platform returns a different match list or error set for the same fixture, then the test shall fail with a diff showing the diverging platform and field.
- REQ-compilerapi-xtest-003 (Where): Where WASM GC is not available in the test environment, the WASM platform test shall be skipped with a clear skip reason rather than failing.

Acceptance:
1) Implementation expectations:
   - compiler-api test module cross-compiled to JVM/JS/Native/WASM.
   - Fixture-driven suite: at least one valid Elm source + valid query (happy), one malformed source (error path), one zero-match query (edge).
   - Assertions compare Result.value, Result.logs, Result.errors structurally (not by reference).
   - Mill commands: ./mill __.compiler-api.test runs all platform variants.
2) Test matrix (mandatory):
   - happy-path: same source+query produces equal MatchView list on all platforms.
   - negative/failure: same malformed source produces equal CompileError entries on all platforms.
   - edge/boundary: empty source + empty query produces equal empty-value Result on all platforms.
   - regression: suite must stay green after any compiler-api source change.
3) User-visible behavior:
   - CI gate; not directly user-visible.
4) Determinism and diagnostics:
   - Match order identical across platforms (asserted explicitly).
   - Failure messages identify which platform diverged and which field differed.

title: webapp: new Scala.js module with Laminar
description:
Context:
- No Scala.js UI module exists for the /try/ playground; we need a CommonScalaJSModule that depends on compiler-api.js and wires Laminar 17.x for the interactive playground.
- This module emits an ESModule that Vite/Astro can import directly for the /try/ page.

EARS requirements:
- REQ-webapp-001 (When): When the webapp module is linked via fullLinkJS, the system shall emit an ESModule bundle (main.js + source map) to docs/public/try/app/ via the writeToDocsPublic Mill task.
- REQ-webapp-002 (If): If moduleDeps includes krueger.compiler-api.js, then the system shall transitively include krueger.core and krueger.trees without requiring separate module declarations.
- REQ-webapp-003 (Where): Where ModuleKind is set to ESModule, the system shall produce output importable by Vite without CommonJS wrapping.

Acceptance:
1) Implementation expectations:
   - New krueger/webapp/ directory with CommonScalaJSModule in mill-build/src/build/Modules.scala and build.mill.yaml.
   - moduleDeps: krueger.compiler-api.js.
   - mvnDeps: com.raquo::laminar::17.x and org.scala-js::scalajs-dom::2.x.
   - ModuleKind: ESModule.
   - Source layout: Main.scala (@JSExportTopLevel entrypoint), App.scala (top-level component), state/AppState.scala, components/ subtree (ActivityBar, EditorGroup, ResultsPanel, MatchesView, TreeView, PrettyQueryView), monaco/ subtree (MonacoFacade, ElmLanguage).
   - fastLinkJS for dev, fullLinkJS for prod.
2) Test matrix (mandatory):
   - happy-path: mill webapp.fastLinkJS completes without linker error.
   - negative/failure: missing compiler-api dependency causes a clear compile error, not a runtime failure.
   - edge/boundary: empty module (no source files) fails at compile time with a clear missing-entrypoint message.
   - regression: adding webapp does not break existing module builds.
3) User-visible behavior:
   - Not directly visible at module-creation stage; unblocks AppState, Monaco, and component sub-tasks.
4) Determinism and diagnostics:
   - Linker output is stable across clean builds.
close_reason: Webapp Scala.js module with Laminar 17.2.0 + scalajs-dom 2.8.0 skeleton. ESModule output verified: fastLinkJS emits main.js with 'export { ... as mount }'. AppInfo + AppInfoSpec establish test harness (2 ZIO tests passing). compiler-api.js transitively pulls in core+trees. Unblocks -2ho, -n0b, -w3k, -z8n, -95s.

title: compiler-api: add WASM linker config / module variant
description:
Context:
- The compiler-api module needs to emit a WebAssembly artifact (in addition to JVM/JS/Native) so webapp-wasm and sites/try-wasm can consume a Wasm GC build.
- Scala.js 1.20.1 (already configured) supports WASM via withExperimentalUseWebAssembly(true) on the linker config.
- This is a linker configuration change, not a source rewrite.

EARS requirements:
- REQ-compilerapi-wasm-001 (When): When the WASM linker variant of compiler-api is built, the system shall emit a main.wasm artifact and an ES-module JS loader glue file alongside it.
- REQ-compilerapi-wasm-002 (If): If withExperimentalUseWebAssembly(true) is set on the ScalaJSLinkerConfig, then the system shall produce a valid Wasm GC module loadable in Chrome 119+, Firefox 120+, and Safari 18.2+.
- REQ-compilerapi-wasm-003 (Where): Where a new CommonScalaJSWasmModule trait or dedicated wasm variant is introduced, the system shall confine the linker override to that variant without altering the standard JS linker config.

Acceptance:
1) Implementation expectations:
   - Enable withExperimentalUseWebAssembly(true) either via a new CommonScalaJSWasmModule trait in mill-build/src/build/Modules.scala or a dedicated compiler-api.wasm variant in build.mill.yaml.
   - Mill task produces both main.wasm and the ES loader (Scala.js emits both automatically when WASM backend is on).
   - No source changes to compiler-api Scala files — only build config.
2) Test matrix (mandatory):
   - happy-path: mill compiler-api.wasm.fullLinkJS completes without error and output dir contains main.wasm + loader .js.
   - negative/failure: if WASM backend unavailable in the Scala.js version, build fails with a clear error rather than silently producing broken output.
   - edge/boundary: wasm artifact is non-trivially sized (>0 bytes, sanity check).
   - regression: standard compiler-api.js linker config unchanged after adding wasm variant.
3) User-visible behavior:
   - Not directly visible; unblocks webapp-wasm and sites/try-wasm.
4) Determinism and diagnostics:
   - Linker output is reproducible across clean builds.
close_reason: CommonScalaJSWasmModule trait added; compiler-api.wasm compiles and links via fastLinkJS+fullLinkJS. Library has no public modules since FFI exports live in webapp-wasm (-5oh); that issue owns the main.wasm artifact verification. js variant unchanged — regression confirmed.

title: feat: Try Krueger interactive playgrounds (Laminar + SvelteKit/WASM)
description:
Context:
- Users can only experiment with Krueger's query DSL by adding a Maven dep and writing Scala. We want zero-install browser experiences on the docs site.
- Ship two flavors: (1) Try Krueger (Laminar) at /try/ inside the existing Astro+Starlight docs, powered by Krueger's Scala.js cross-build; (2) Try Krueger (WASM) at /try-wasm/ as a SvelteKit static site consuming a WebAssembly build of the compiler.
- Both UIs share a new krueger.compiler-api module (PureLogic-backed contract), proving the compiler component can be reused across JVM, Scala.js, Scala Native, and WASM targets.
- Editor: Monaco in both with an Elm Monarch tokenizer. Output pane: VS Code sidebar-inspired (Matches, CST tree, AST tree, Canonical query echo). Full-bleed pages; new top-nav override on Starlight.
- Full plan: /Users/damian/.claude/plans/lets-make-updates-to-crystalline-sonnet.md and GitHub tracking issue (linked once filed).

EARS requirements:
- REQ-trykrueger-001 (When): When a user navigates to /try/ or /try-wasm/, the system shall render a split editor (source + query) with a VS Code-style output panel showing matches, CST, AST, and canonical query echo.
- REQ-trykrueger-002 (If): If the source or query is malformed, then the system shall surface a structured error (CompileError ADT) without freezing the editor.
- REQ-trykrueger-003 (Where): Where a browser lacks Wasm GC support, /try-wasm/ shall show a fallback banner directing the user to /try/.
- REQ-trykrueger-004 (While): While both UIs are consuming the same CompilerComponent, identical source+query inputs shall produce structurally equal match/CST/AST outputs.
- REQ-trykrueger-005 (When): When the user opens any docs page, the system shall display a top nav with a 'Try' entry linking to both playground flavors.

Acceptance:
1) Implementation expectations:
   - New krueger.compiler-api module (JVM/JS/Native/WASM) with PureLogic-backed CompilerComponent trait.
   - New krueger.webapp Scala.js module (Laminar UI) emitted to docs/public/try/app/.
   - New krueger.webapp-wasm Scala.js module (WASM linker) emitted to sites/try-wasm/static/wasm/.
   - New sites/try-wasm SvelteKit project (adapter-static) wired to the WASM bundle.
   - Astro Header override + full-bleed /try/ page.
   - Mill tasks webapp.writeToDocsPublic and webapp-wasm.writeToWasmSite.
   - CI stitches SvelteKit build into docs/dist/try-wasm/.
2) Test matrix (mandatory):
   - happy-path: paste Elm + query, matches/CST/AST/pretty all render in both flavors.
   - negative/failure: malformed source and malformed query each surface readable CompileError without crashing signals.
   - edge/boundary: empty source, empty query, very large input, zero-match query.
   - regression: cross-platform compiler-api.test asserts JVM/JS/Native/WASM return structurally equal Result values for a fixed fixture.
3) User-visible behavior:
   - Unit tests for AppState (Laminar signals) and SvelteKit krueger.ts wrapper.
   - Follow-up Playwright E2E (separate issues, non-blocking for initial ship).
4) Determinism and diagnostics:
   - Match order asserted explicitly.
   - Logs and errors surfaced via PureLogic Result envelope.
   - FFI envelope { ok, value?, logs, errors } consumed unchanged by SvelteKit.

title: trees/query: pretty-print Query via Kindling FastShowPretty
description:
## Context
Add a canonical pretty-printer for `Query` using Kindling `FastShowPretty`, so a parsed query AST can be rendered back to query text in a deterministic form.

The rendered output should support roundtrip equivalence where syntactic whitespace differences are intentionally ignored (because parser/AST does not preserve whitespace).

## EARS requirements
- When a valid `Query` AST is pretty-printed, the system shall emit a deterministic canonical query string.
- If two input query strings are semantically equivalent but differ only in whitespace/trivia, then the system shall roundtrip them to equivalent ASTs and canonical output.
- Where pretty-print output is reparsed, the system shall preserve query semantics (patterns, captures, predicates, alternation/anchors/quantifiers/negated fields) without loss.

## Acceptance Criteria
1) Implement pretty-print support for `Query` using Kindling `FastShowPretty`.
2) Ensure every supported query construct is rendered (node patterns, fields, child patterns, captures, predicates, alternation `[]`, anchors `.`, quantifiers `?/*/+`, negated fields `!field`).
3) Add roundtrip tests:
   - happy path: `parse -> pretty -> parse` yields equivalent AST,
   - negative path: malformed input still fails parser (no pretty fallback),
   - edge path: whitespace/trivia-normalization cases roundtrip equivalently,
   - regression path: existing supported query features remain stable under pretty-print roundtrip.
4) Document canonical formatting expectations (e.g. spacing, ordering where relevant) and verify whitespace is treated as non-significant for equality checks.
5) Include unit tests and at least one BDD/integration scenario proving end-to-end roundtrip behavior.
close_reason: Implemented QueryPretty (Kindlings FastShowPretty), unit roundtrip + whitespace tests, BDD canonical roundtrip, README; pushed in e411732.

title: parser: multiple top-level value declarations drop to one
description:
Discovered while adding QueryableTree[CstNode] tests. A module source with three adjacent value declarations only produces one CstValueDeclaration in CstModule.declarations; the second and third are silently dropped.

Repro (any of these only yields one value declaration):

  module App exposing (..)

  import Html

  x = 1
  y = 2
  z = 3

Or with blank lines between them:

  module App exposing (..)

  import Html

  x = 1

  y = 2

  z = 3

Expected: CstModule.declarations.size == 3.

Likely in ModuleParser / DeclarationParser — probably the top-level decl loop stops after the first successful decl instead of looping until EOF.

Priority medium — blocks any BDD query test that wants to iterate over a handful of declarations, and is likely to bite real Elm users fast.

Part of the core parser, not the trees work — file under parser.
close_reason: Closed

title: trees: NodeTypeName as a validated neotype
description:
Tighten QueryableTree[T].nodeType so it returns a validated NodeTypeName rather than a raw String.

Use neotype (https://github.com/kitlangton/neotype) for the wrapper. Validation rules:
- Non-null (guaranteed by opaque-type ergonomics).
- Non-empty.
- Not all whitespace.

Touch points when swapping:
- QueryableTree[T].nodeType signature.
- ToyTree's given instance.

Lives in krueger.core (which depends on krueger.trees, not the other way around).

title: trees: matcher engine with pluggable predicates
description:
Run a Query against any T given a QueryableTree[T] and yield all matches.

API:
- def matches[T: QueryableTree](query: Query, root: T, registry: PredicateRegistry = PredicateRegistry.default): LazyList[Match[T]]
- class PredicateRegistry(predicates: Map[String, PredicateImpl])
- trait PredicateImpl: def evaluate(args: List[PredicateArg], captures: Map[String, Any]): Boolean
- PredicateRegistry.default includes '#eq?' and '#match?'

Algorithm: depth-first traversal over the root, at each node attempt to match the top-level pattern; for a matching node, collect field children via QueryableTree.fields, recursively match sub-patterns; filter candidate matches through predicates; return LazyList so callers can short-circuit.

Acceptance: unit tests over the ToyTree cover node, field, capture, wildcard, #eq?, and #match?; empty-query, no-match, and many-match cases all tested.
close_reason: Closed

title: trees: S-expression query parser (Parsley)
description:
Parse tree-sitter-style query strings into the v1 query AST using Parsley (already a project dependency).

Supported syntax for v1:
- Node pattern: (NodeType)
- Field pattern: (NodeType field_name: (ChildType))
- Capture: @name appended to any pattern, e.g. (NodeType) @decl
- Wildcard: _ and (_)
- Predicate clause at top level: (#eq? @a @b) or (#match? @a "regex")
- Whitespace and line comments (;; ...)

Validation: the parser resolves NodeType strings against a caller-supplied 'known types' set and emits a helpful error if a name is unknown. This keeps runtime errors near the query boundary.

Acceptance: round-trip tests for a canonical set of queries; parser errors point to the offending column; malformed queries return Failure, not exceptions.

title: trees: query AST (Pattern, Capture, Predicate, Match)
description:
Design and implement the data model the parser produces and the matcher consumes.

ADT sketch:
- sealed trait Pattern
- case class NodePattern(nodeType: String, fieldPatterns: List[FieldPattern], capture: Option[String]) extends Pattern
- case class FieldPattern(name: String, pattern: Pattern)
- case class WildcardPattern(capture: Option[String]) extends Pattern   // for tree-sitter '_'
- sealed trait Predicate
- case class EqPredicate(left: PredicateArg, right: PredicateArg) extends Predicate
- case class MatchPredicate(arg: PredicateArg, regex: String) extends Predicate
- case class Query(root: Pattern, predicates: List[Predicate])
- case class Match[T](root: T, captures: Map[String, T])

Keep alternation, quantifiers, anchors, and negation OUT of v1 — document them as TODO comments with links to the v2 epic.

Acceptance: ADT compiles on all platforms; round-trip tests (build AST by hand, pretty-print, re-parse) planned for the parser issue.

title: trees: QueryableTree[T] typeclass
description:
Define the generic typeclass that every tree type implements to participate in the query engine.

Signature (v1, intentionally minimal):

trait QueryableTree[T]:
    def nodeType(t: T): String
    def children(t: T): Seq[T]
    def fields(t: T): Map[String, Seq[T]]
    def text(t: T): Option[String]

title: Parser: support value declarations with type annotations (indentation-sensitive parsing)
description:
Elm's canonical form for an annotated value binding is two consecutive top-level lines:

    foo : Int
    foo = 42

The current Krueger parser cannot parse this. When typeExpression finishes parsing `Int`, its inner `appType = atomType <~> many(atomType)` greedily matches the next `foo` as a lowercase typeVariable (via `lowerName`), consuming the identifier that should have started the next value declaration. The valueDeclaration that follows the annotation then fails because its lowerName has no input.

This is not an atomic-backtracking issue — the type-expression parse genuinely SUCCEEDS with the wrong reading. Fixing it requires indentation-sensitive parsing: the grammar must treat a column-1 (or less-indented-than-type-expr) identifier on a new line as ENDING the type expression, not continuing it.

Scope

Two scenarios were removed from the integration-test suite in the PR that introduced it, pending this work:

- declarations.feature — "Value declaration with annotation"
- lowering.feature — "Lowering surfaces type annotations"

Annotation lowering is still regression-protected via a direct CST unit test
(CstLoweringSpec.scala → "lowerDeclaration carries a value's type annotation onto the AST"), so only the PARSER path is unprotected.

Acceptance criteria

1. `module M exposing (..)\nfoo : Int\nfoo = 42\n` parses via Krueger.parseCst / parseAst without consuming the second `foo` as part of the type expression.
2. The two cucumber scenarios listed above are restored in krueger/itest/resources/features/ and pass.
3. A dedicated ModuleParserSpec test is added that parses an annotated value decl and asserts both the annotation and body on the CstValueDeclaration.
4. No regression in the existing 70 unit tests or 44 cucumber scenarios.

Design options (non-binding)

- Use parsley's pos to snapshot the column at the start of the type expression, and in many(atomType) / many("->" *> appType) bail via lookAhead when the next token's column is ≤ the start column. Matches Elm's offside rule without a full pre-pass.
- Introduce an "indented" combinator utility and thread it through DeclarationParser.typeExpression (and eventually expression parsing). More invasive but pays off for let/case bodies too.
- Pre-tokenisation layout pass that injects `{`, `}`, `;` tokens at block boundaries (Haskell-style). Largest change, most faithful to Elm.

Whichever path is taken, criterion #2 is the ground truth.
close_reason: Implemented indentation-sensitive type-expression tails for annotated value declarations and restored parser/lowering coverage.

title: e2e: Playwright smoke tests for /try/ and /try-wasm/
description:
Context:
- Both playgrounds need end-to-end smoke tests to catch regressions in the deployed static output that unit/integration tests cannot catch (Monaco initialization, WASM load, cross-browser rendering).
- These are explicitly non-blocking for initial ship but are required follow-up work per the plan.

EARS requirements:
- REQ-e2e-001 (When): When a Playwright test navigates to /try/ and types a valid Elm source and query, the system shall display a non-zero match count in the Matches panel within a reasonable timeout.
- REQ-e2e-002 (When): When a Playwright test navigates to /try-wasm/ in a Wasm-GC-capable browser, the system shall initialize the playground and display match results for the same source+query used in REQ-e2e-001.
- REQ-e2e-003 (If): If the browser used for /try-wasm/ tests lacks Wasm GC support, then the test shall assert the fallback banner is visible rather than expecting the playground to initialize.

Acceptance:
1) Implementation expectations:
   - Playwright test suite (e2e/ or sites/try-wasm/e2e/) covering /try/ and /try-wasm/ routes.
   - Tests run against the production static build (npm run preview or Pages artifact).
   - Test matrix mirrors the verification steps in the plan: type source + query, assert match count appears, assert CST tree renders, assert AST tree renders, assert canonical query echo appears.
   - Wasm GC fallback test uses a browser profile or feature stub that disables Wasm GC.
2) Test matrix (mandatory):
   - happy-path: /try/ playground fully functional end-to-end (source → query → matches → CST → AST → echo).
   - happy-path: /try-wasm/ playground fully functional end-to-end in Chrome 119+.
   - negative/failure: malformed query on /try/ shows error message without page crash.
   - negative/failure: malformed query on /try-wasm/ shows error message without page crash.
   - edge/boundary: /try-wasm/ fallback banner visible in Wasm-GC-unsupported browser profile.
   - regression: match output on /try/ and /try-wasm/ is identical for the same source+query input.
3) User-visible behavior:
   - Not user-visible directly; CI gate for deployed Pages artifact quality.
4) Determinism and diagnostics:
   - Tests assert match count and panel content, not just page load (no trivially-passing smoke tests).
   - Failure output identifies which route, which panel, and what was expected vs. actual.

title: trees: broaden predicate/directive compatibility surface
description:
## Context
Extend predicate/directive compatibility toward tree-sitter parity while maintaining clear unsupported behavior rules.

## EARS requirements
- When supported predicates/directives are used, the system shall parse/evaluate them per documented semantics.
- If unsupported predicate/directive forms are used, then the system shall fail with explicit unsupported diagnostics.
- Where directives affect matching metadata, the system shall keep behavior deterministic and testable.

## Acceptance Criteria
1) Define supported vs unsupported predicate/directive set explicitly.
2) Add parser/matcher handling and tests for selected new supported forms.
3) Add negative tests for unsupported forms with stable diagnostics.
4) Add BDD scenarios and step-language extensions where needed for directive assertions.
5) Test matrix coverage: happy path, negative/failure, edge/boundary, and regression cases.
close_reason: Expanded predicate compatibility with #not-eq? and #not-match?, added explicit unsupported-directive diagnostics (e.g. #set!), extended unit + BDD coverage for positive/negative/edge flows, and documented supported/unsupported forms.

title: trees: anchor semantics for sibling/positional constraints
description:
Introduce anchor semantics aligned with tree-sitter-style positional constraints.

EARS requirements:
- When anchors are used in valid positions, the system shall enforce the documented positional constraint.
- If anchors are used in invalid contexts, then the system shall fail with actionable diagnostics.
- Where anchor rules affect ordering and adjacency, the system shall assert deterministic outcomes.

Acceptance:
1) Parser validates anchor placement and reports invalid placement failures clearly.
2) Matcher enforces anchor semantics with deterministic ordering behavior.
3) Add positive, negative, edge, and regression tests (unit + BDD).
4) Add/expand step language for ordering/adjacency assertions where required.
close_reason: Implemented anchor semantics for sibling adjacency using '.' between unfielded child patterns, added parser validation with explicit invalid-placement diagnostics, extended matcher adjacency enforcement, and added unit + BDD positive/negative/edge coverage with deterministic behavior assertions.

title: trees: negated field constraints support (!field)
description:
Add support for negated field constraints in node patterns.

EARS requirements:
- When negated field constraints are used validly, the system shall enforce field absence semantics.
- If negated fields conflict with positive field constraints, then the system shall fail with explicit diagnostics.
- Where optional/empty field representations differ by tree type, the system shall define and test expected behavior.

Acceptance:
1) Parser accepts valid !field forms and rejects invalid/conflicting forms.
2) Matcher enforces negated field semantics with deterministic results.
3) Add positive, negative, edge, and regression tests (unit + BDD).
4) Expand step language where needed for field-absence assertions.
close_reason: Implemented !field negated field constraints with parser support, explicit conflicting-constraint diagnostics, matcher enforcement for absent/empty field semantics, and expanded unit + BDD positive/negative/edge/regression coverage.

title: trees: alternation patterns support (tree-sitter-style [])
description:
Add tree-sitter-style alternation patterns in the query language.

EARS requirements:
- When alternation syntax is valid, the system shall parse and match any branch deterministically.
- If alternation syntax is malformed, then the system shall fail with clear diagnostics.
- Where alternation interacts with captures/fields, the system shall preserve deterministic capture semantics.

Acceptance:
1) Parser accepts alternation forms and rejects malformed alternation with actionable errors.
2) Matcher evaluates alternation branches correctly with deterministic results.
3) Include unit + BDD positive, negative, and edge tests.
4) Expand step language where needed to express alternation assertions clearly.
close_reason: Implemented tree-sitter-style [] alternation patterns with deterministic branch-order matching, parser validation for malformed empty alternations, and expanded unit + BDD positive/negative/edge coverage including field and capture interactions.

title: trees: quantifier support for query patterns (* + ?)
description:
Add quantifier support for query patterns with clear semantics and diagnostics.

EARS requirements:
- When quantifiers are used on supported pattern forms, the system shall match according to documented semantics.
- If quantifiers are applied in unsupported/malformed positions, then the system shall fail with clear diagnostics.
- Where quantifiers create ambiguity, the system shall define and assert deterministic behavior.

Acceptance:
1) Parser supports valid quantifier placements and rejects invalid placements.
2) Matcher behavior for *, +, ? is documented and tested, including boundary cases.
3) Include positive, negative, edge, and regression tests in unit + BDD layers.
4) Expand Gherkin step language where needed for repeated-capture assertions.
close_reason: Implemented child-pattern quantifier support (?, *, +) with deterministic matcher semantics, parser validation for invalid quantifier placement, and expanded unit + BDD positive/negative/edge coverage including boundary behavior for optional and repeated matches.

title: trees: typed DSL surface with typed captures
description:
## Context
Add a typed Scala DSL surface on top of `NodeByName` so query construction and capture retrieval can be checked at compile time.

Goal shape:
`query[CstNode].node[CstValueDeclaration].field("name", node[CstName].capture("n")).capture("decl")`

## EARS requirements
- When the typed DSL is used with valid node/capture names, the system shall produce compile-time-safe query construction.
- If DSL usage is invalid (unknown node/capture shape), then the system shall fail at compile time with actionable type errors.
- Where typed captures and runtime matcher output intersect, the system shall preserve deterministic mapping semantics.

## Acceptance Criteria
1) Implement typed query builder primitives aligned with `NodeByName` and `QueryableTree` constraints.
2) Expose typed capture accessors with no runtime casts for successful compile-time paths.
3) Test matrix requirements:
   - happy path: DSL queries compile and execute with typed capture retrieval,
   - negative path: invalid node/capture references fail compilation,
   - edge path: nested fields/alternation/quantifier interactions remain deterministic,
   - regression path: existing string-based query APIs continue unchanged.
4) Include compile-time assertion tests and runtime parity checks against equivalent string queries.

title: trees: Mirror/Hearth-derived QueryableTree instances
description:
## Context
Investigate macro-backed auto-derivation so sealed ADTs can obtain `QueryableTree` instances with minimal hand-written boilerplate.

Candidate libraries:
- Hearth (https://github.com/MateuszKubuszok/hearth)
- Kindlings (https://github.com/kubuszok/kindlings)

## EARS requirements
- When derivation is enabled for supported ADTs, the system shall produce a `QueryableTree` instance equivalent to the hand-written contract.
- If derivation encounters unsupported ADT shapes, then the system shall fail with explicit diagnostics instead of silent fallback.
- Where macro-generated field/children ordering can vary, the system shall define and preserve deterministic ordering semantics.

## Acceptance Criteria
1) Execute a spike for deriving `NodeTypes`, `resolve`, `fields`, and `children` metadata from mirrors.
2) Compare Hearth-only vs Hearth+Kindlings and document chosen path with rationale.
3) Test matrix requirements:
   - happy path: derived instances match hand-written behavior for reference ADTs,
   - negative path: unsupported ADTs emit actionable diagnostics,
   - edge path: optional/repeated fields and sealed hierarchies preserve deterministic behavior,
   - regression path: existing hand-written instances continue to work unchanged.
4) Provide unit and integration verification strategy suitable for future implementation issue slicing.

title: trees: NodeTypes type member + match-type narrowing
description:
## Context
Augment `QueryableTree[T]` with a type-level closed universe of node types to support safer compile-time narrowing and future typed DSL ergonomics.

Current gate: hold until there is sufficient v1 consumer evidence that the additional type-level constraints do not add unacceptable friction.

## EARS requirements
- When type-level node narrowing is introduced, the system shall preserve existing v1 runtime query behavior for current consumers.
- If narrowing or resolver APIs introduce usability friction, then the system shall defer rollout and keep the issue gated.
- Where compile-time narrowing is enabled, the system shall provide deterministic and verifiable type-resolution behavior.

## Acceptance Criteria
1) Introduce `type NodeTypes <: T` and typed resolver shape (`resolve` + `NodeByName`) behind a migration-safe design.
2) Define explicit gate criteria: at least two independent v1 consumers and >=2 sprints of v1 usage evidence before activation.
3) Test matrix requirements:
   - happy path: valid name-to-type narrowing compiles and resolves,
   - negative path: unknown names / unsupported narrowing fail clearly,
   - edge path: overlapping or ambiguous names resolve deterministically,
   - regression path: existing v1 parser/matcher behavior remains unchanged.
4) Verification includes compile-time checks plus runtime compatibility tests for existing v1 flows.
notes: Reopened: holding off on type-level narrowing changes until we validate they do not add unwanted friction.
close_reason: won't do
